FYP Daily Paper Reminder

Variational Graph Auto-Encoders

Tuesday, 14 July 2026  •  Paper #1  •  Cycle #1

MUST READ TODAY    CURATED READING LIST
Authors Thomas N. Kipf, Max Welling
Venue arXiv / NIPS Workshop on Bayesian Deep Learning 2016
Year 2016  

▶ TL;DR

Introduces Graph Autoencoder (GAE) and Variational Graph Autoencoder (VGAE) — an encoder-decoder framework that learns latent node representations and reconstructs the adjacency matrix. High reconstruction error = anomaly.

Key Findings

  • GCN encoder produces latent embeddings; inner product decoder reconstructs adjacency
  • VGAE adds a variational (Bayesian) inference layer for better regularization
  • Reconstruction error is a natural anomaly score — no labels needed
  • Works on graphs with node features AND purely structural graphs

Why Read This For Your FYP

This is your Stage 1 anomaly detector (unsupervised baseline). Train GAE on NORMAL Kubernetes traffic only — then flag high reconstruction-error graphs as attacks. Only 4 pages — read it today.

Reading Focus

The entire paper is short (4 pages). Focus on Equation 1 (ELBO objective), Figure 1 (architecture), and Table 1 (link prediction results). The anomaly detection application is not in this paper — that's YOUR novel application.

Read the Paper

arXivPublisherResearchGateGitHub Issue

Today's Reading Checklist

Read the abstract and introduction
Study the sections highlighted in Reading Focus above
Note 2-3 ways this differs from your project (for Related Work)
Add BibTeX entry to references.bib
Add 1-2 sentences to ch03_literature_review.tex
Update weekly_progress.md — Master Paper Reading List
